issues.description: `format_token_count` silently drops the tens and ones digits when the hundreds digit of the remainder is zero. For example, `format_token_count(1001)` returns `"1,000"` instead of `"1,001"`, because `(1001 % 1000) / 100 == 0` takes the else branch that hardcodes `,000`.
issues.suggestion: Replace the branching logic with a single format path: `format!("{thousands},{:03}", n % 1_000)` — the `:03` already zero-pads correctly. Remove the `remainder` variable and the `if/else` entirely.

issues.description: FR-1a specifies `k`/`↑` should only scroll up "when scrolled" (i.e., when the pane is already pinned away from bottom). The implementation unconditionally calls `scroll_up(1)` on Up/k even when at bottom (`log_scroll_offset == 0`), which means pressing Up once will pin the view and show `[paused]`. The spec intends PgUp/Home as the entry points to scroll mode, with j/k for fine navigation once already scrolled.
issues.suggestion: Guard the Up/k handler: `if !self.is_scrolled_to_bottom() { self.scroll_up(1); }`. Same consideration for Down/j: the spec says "only when scrolled", so guard with `if !self.is_scrolled_to_bottom() { self.scroll_down(1); }`.

issues.description: The scroll calculation `total.saturating_sub(inner_height).saturating_sub(log_scroll_offset)` counts logical lines, but the Paragraph widget has `.wrap(Wrap { trim: false })` which makes long lines occupy multiple visual rows. A single wrapped log line that occupies 3 visual rows will cause the ratatui scroll offset to be off by 2 rows, making the viewport jump or cut off content. This is pre-existing (wrap was already enabled), but the new scrollback feature makes it user-visible — scrolling up by 1 may skip past wrapped content.
issues.suggestion: Either remove `.wrap(Wrap { trim: false })` from the log pane (truncating long lines, which is common for log viewers) or switch to manually slicing the `lines` vector to the visible window and rendering without `.scroll()`, so the scroll math operates on logical entries rather than fighting with ratatui's internal wrap counting.

issues.description: The test stage ✓/✗ check requires BOTH `all_passed == true` AND `ci_status == "passed"` for a checkmark. The spec says non-passed `ci_status` triggers ✗ but doesn't require the conjunction. If the API returns `all_passed: true` with `ci_status: "success"` (a plausible alternative value), the result would incorrectly show ✗. The double-condition is overly strict and fragile to API naming.
issues.suggestion: Consider using `all_passed` alone as the primary signal, or at minimum `ci_status != "failed"` rather than `ci_status == "passed"`.

issues.description: FR-3b spec text says timestamp is "followed by a single space" but the implementation uses two spaces (`format!("{time_str}  ")`). The spec's own example also shows two spaces, so the spec is internally inconsistent. The implementation follows the example, which is reasonable, but worth noting.
issues.suggestion: No action needed if the example is authoritative. If strict spec-text compliance is desired, change to a single space.
